what_it_does: This file is a flat JSON ledger that maps screen-specific UI addresses (like ui://toolbar/run) to a region, a semantic feature tag, an optional code reference, and a registered boolean, across a set of HTML screen artifacts.
observations: Every entry has a screen file, an address, a region, a feature, an optional code field, and a registered flag.
Most UI addresses use the ui:// scheme; a few use run:// and are marked registered: false.
Many code fields are null even though the entry is registered: true.
The same address can appear multiple times within one screen with different features (e.g., ui://canvas/portal-window).
The same code reference is reused across screens (e.g., canvas/app/src/regions/Toolbar.tsx, canvas/app/src/NodeShape.tsx:NodeShapeUtil, runtime/suite.py:run).
Feature names mix terse coded tags (RUN-run, RHM-modes, NODE-types) with prose descriptions ('The memo gate mechanism', 'A content node block in the palette rack').
Addresses are often nested paths under a region (ui://toolbar/presence/dial).
The file is truncated; the full entry set is not visible.
standouts: Recurring central code hooks: Toolbar.tsx, NodeShape.tsx:NodeShapeUtil, runtime/suite.py:run, runtime/suite.py:set_mode.
RHM (presence / mode) surfaces appear heavily across toolbar, chat, settings, and twin screens.
run://demo/ask and run://system/model_of_tim entries are explicitly unregistered.
Inbox lanes are tied to runtime/suite.py:inbox_lanes and review_verdicts.
Inspector model field links NodeConfigForm to runtime/suite.py:available_models.
conventions: Feature tags often use a hyphenated code pattern (AREA-thing).
Code field may list a React component, a Python function, or a combination separated by ' / '.
registered: true is the default for ui:// entries; run:// entries are registered: false.
concerns: High number of null code fields weakens the design-to-code traceability goal.
Duplicate addresses within a single screen make the ledger ambiguous about what each address uniquely identifies.
Inconsistent feature naming makes automated grouping harder.
The truncation prevents assessing completeness or checking for further duplicates.
notes: A screen-to-code element ledger that records which UI addresses correspond to regions, features, and implementation hooks, with many entries still lacking code references.
questions: What does registered mean precisely, and why are run:// entries unregistered?
Why are some addresses repeated within the same screen with different features?
Is each address intended to be unique globally, unique per screen, or intentionally multi-mapped?
How are null code fields expected to be filled over time?
How many total entries does the full file contain?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears intended to map every addressable UI element to its implementation substrate; in practice it is a flat list with mixed granularity, duplicate addresses, and many missing code links, so the traceability goal is only partially realized.
intention_signals: The file seems to be trying to establish a single shared registry where design screens, product specs, and engineering code can all refer to the same element addresses and locate the corresponding implementation.
novelty: It uses URI-like addresses (ui://, run://) for design-system elements and an explicit registered flag to indicate whether a design artifact is currently grounded in code.
contribution: It contributes a structured, addressable inventory of UI elements and their intended code or design substrates, which can serve as a lookup and traceability layer even where code links are not yet populated.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON ledger mapping screen-specific UI addresses to regions, semantic feature tags, optional code references, and a registered flag for a design system.
intention_for_embedding: To create a canonical, queryable map between design screens and their underlying code or design substrates.
